title: ServeMux Patterns, Precedence, Path Values, and Route Safety · Execution Trace: Head Options Redirects
summary: A Go 1.26 service mixes legacy prefix routes with method and wildcard patterns, conflicts at startup, misroutes escaped slashes, and assumes slash redirects still use a permanent status. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo head options redirects increment in validated ServeMux route and precedence table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Account for GET matching HEAD, method-specific 405 Allow responses, OPTIONS behavior, host routes, and Go 1.26 temporary slash redirects.
Use Request.PathValue while distinguishing segment unescaping, escaped slash data, raw query, cleaned paths, and invalid identifiers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
